steps.title: Identify the key reversal
steps.content: The passage contrasts the initial assumption—that T. navigans flew primarily like other pterosaurs—with new skeletal evidence showing short wings, long hind legs, a long neck, and an oversized head that would make sustained flight difficult and walking comfortable.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
flew for shorter distances and spent more time walking than researchers previously thought. directly captures this reversal: it states T. navigans “flew for shorter distances and spent more time walking than researchers previously thought,” which reflects the evidence-based shift from flight-focused to walking-focused locomotion.

The other three contradict the skeleton's characteristics or introduce comparisons unsupported by the passage.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
